div.p: Window inflation during recovery, deflation on exit
Key Insight: Inflation allows new transmissions during recovery. Deflation prevents burst on exit.
ssthresh = cwnd / 2 retransmit lost segment cwnd = ssthresh + 3*MSS recovery_mode = true recover_seq = snd_nxt
Why +3 MSS? Each dup ACK means 1 segment left the network. 3 dup ACKs = 3 segments delivered, so inflate window to allow new transmission.
Window inflates as dup ACKs arrive, allowing new data transmission during recovery.
Initial cwnd: 10 MSS (14600 bytes) ssthresh: 5 MSS (7300 bytes) Entry cwnd: 8 MSS (11680 bytes) After dup #4: 9 MSS After dup #5: 10 MSS After dup #6: 11 MSS
Window deflates back to ssthresh, entering congestion avoidance.

ul.li: Linear growth: cwnd += MSS²/cwnd per RTT
